name: MIRROR: Converging Cognitive Principles as Computational Mechanisms for AI Reasoning
content: Multiple cognitive theories -- Global Workspace Theory, reconstructive episodic memory, inner speech, and complementary learning systems -- converge on a shared set of architectural principles: parallel specialized processing, integrative synthesis into a bounded unified representation, and reconstructive rather than accumulative maintenance. We test whether these converging principles provide computational advantages when implemented in AI systems. MIRROR operationalizes each principle as a con
summary: 通过将人类认知原则应用于AI系统，MIRROR展示了在多轮对话中维护约束的能力显著提升，证明了这些原则对增强人工智能性能的潜在价值。
key_insights: MIRROR成功地将人类认知原理转化为有效的计算机制，提高了多个语言模型的表现。
重建性合成对于所有测试的语言模型都有助益，表明这种处理方式是互补性的。
实验结果支持了理论预测，在高注意力负荷下，集成信息的可用性对表现的影响最大。
xihe_relevance: MIRROR的研究成果可能为曦和项目提供有价值的方向和见解。
